title: Principles and rules for the structure and drafting of ISO and IEC documents

elements.description: Title page of an ISO or IEC document. Includes standard number, document type, English title (and French title for ISO documents), ICS classification code, edition, and issue date. No CCS code (CCS is a Chinese national classification system not used by ISO/IEC).
elements.notes: Standard number format: ISO XXXXX:YYYY or IEC XXXXX:YYYY or ISO/IEC XXXXX:YYYY. No CCS field. Title in English is primary; for ISO documents a French title is also published but optional in drafts.

elements.description: List of all clauses and subclauses with page numbers. Also lists annexes (indicating normative or informative), bibliography, and index if present.
elements.notes: Optional. When present, lists all headings with page numbers. Annexes labeled (normative) or (informative).

elements.description: Required element with ISO/IEC-standard boilerplate. Must identify the responsible ISO/IEC committee and subcommittee, list other parts in the series (if any), identify predecessor documents (if superseding), include the patent declaration statement, and reference the ISO/IEC Directives Part 2 as the drafting rules.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. ISO boilerplate is mandatory: (1) name the TC/SC; (2) relationship to other parts of the series; (3) supersession statement; (4) patent declaration per ISO/IEC Directives Part 1 Annex F; (5) reference to Directives Part 2. Do not include technical content.

elements.description: Optional element giving specific information or commentary about the technical content and the reasons for preparing the document. No normative requirements. Subclauses numbered 0.1, 0.2, etc. Patent declarations are placed here if applicable.
elements.notes: Optional. No requirements. Use clause number prefix 0 if subdivided (0.1, 0.2...). ISO patent declaration template may be placed here.

elements.description: ALWAYS Clause 1. Defines the subject and limits the applicability of the document. Shall be succinct so that it can be used as a summary for bibliographic purposes. States what the document specifies, establishes, gives, or provides guidance on.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. Always Clause 1. Shall: 'This document specifies / establishes / gives / provides guidelines for'. State scope concisely. Can state what is NOT covered.

elements.description: Always Clause 2. Heading is ALWAYS present (required shell). Content is conditional: lists normatively referenced documents when present, or includes the placeholder sentence when none. For dated references: only cited edition applies. For undated: latest edition applies.
elements.empty_text_en: There are no normative references in this document.
elements.notes: REQUIRED SHELL, CONDITIONAL CONTENT. Clause 2 heading always present. Standard leading sentence when references exist: 'The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document.' If none: placeholder sentence.

elements.description: Always Clause 3. Heading always present (required shell). Content conditional: list own terms, cite external vocabulary standard, or use placeholder if none. Three cases for the preamble per §6.3: (a) own terms only, (b) external reference only, (c) both.
elements.empty_text_en: No terms and definitions are listed in this document.

intro_preambles.own_only_en: For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply.
intro_preambles.external_only_en: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in [REF] apply.
intro_preambles.both_en: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in [REF] and the following apply.

elements.notes: REQUIRED SHELL, CONDITIONAL CONTENT. Clause 3. Three preamble cases: own / external / both. If none: 'No terms and definitions are listed in this document.' ISO/IEC Vocabulary IEV (IEC 60050) is the primary reference vocabulary.

elements.description: Lists symbols, units, and abbreviations used in the document. Symbols listed in order of appearance or alphabetically. Abbreviations expanded. Can be split into separate clauses when extensive.
elements.notes: Optional. Usually Clause 4. May be titled 'Symbols', 'Abbreviations', 'Symbols and abbreviated terms', or 'Units' depending on content.

elements.description: The main normative technical content. Clause 4 onwards (or 5 if Symbols clause present). Use 'shall' for requirements, 'should' for recommendations, 'may' for permissions, 'can' for possibility/capability, 'need not' for non-requirement, 'must' for external constraints only.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. Modal verbs per ISO/IEC Directives: shall / should / may / can / need not. Do not use 'must' for internal requirements — 'must' is reserved for external (e.g. legal) obligations.

elements.description: Normative annexes contain requirements that are an integral part of the document. Labeled Annex A, Annex B... Designated '(normative)' immediately after the annex heading.
elements.notes: Optional. Must be labeled '(normative)'. Clauses within annex: A.1, A.2, A.1.1... Do not use letters I, O (reserved for informative annexes in some systems), but ISO/IEC in practice uses all capitals.

elements.description: Informative annexes provide supplementary information only. Labeled '(informative)' after heading. Cannot contain requirements (no 'shall'). Lettered continuing from normative annexes.
elements.notes: Optional. Must be labeled '(informative)'. No 'shall' statements. Numbered continuing from normative annexes.

elements.description: Required section for informative references and further reading. Appears after all annexes. Documents cited informatively (not normatively). Uses sequential numeric labels [1], [2], etc. References to ISO/IEC documents listed first, then other international standards, then national standards.
elements.notes: Required (present at end). Informative references listed with [N] numbering. ISO/IEC Directives do not prescribe citation format beyond numbering; follow ISO 690 or ISO/IEC Directives Annex D.

elements.description: Alphabetical index of terms and concepts with clause references. Only included in large standards with extensive terminology.
elements.notes: Optional. Informative. Use only for documents large enough to warrant a findability aid.

element_ordering_rules.required_shell_note: Clause heading always present. Content conditional: placeholder sentence used when no content. Source: ISO/IEC Directives Part 2:2021 §6.2, §6.3.

numbering_rules.annexes_labeled: A, B, C, ... (consecutive Latin capital letters)
